obligations.requirement_text: Providers and deployers shall not place on the market, put into service, or use AI systems that deploy subliminal techniques beyond a person's consciousness or purposefully manipulative or deceptive techniques, with the objective or with the effect of materially distorting the behaviour of a person or a group of persons, causing or reasonably likely to cause significant harm.
obligations.scope: Providers and deployers of AI systems placed on the market, put into service, or used in the Union

obligations.requirement_text: Providers and deployers shall not place on the market, put into service, or use AI systems that evaluate or classify natural persons or groups of persons based on social behaviour or personal or personality characteristics, where the social score leads to detrimental or unfavourable treatment in unrelated social contexts or to treatment that is unjustified or disproportionate to the gravity of the social behaviour.
obligations.scope: Providers and deployers of AI systems placed on the market, put into service, or used in the Union, whether provided or used by public or private persons

obligations.source_paragraph: Article 5(1)(d) AI Act (as interpreted in paragraph 188)
obligations.requirement_text: The prohibition in Article 5(1)(d) AI Act applies to both providers and deployers of AI systems, each within their respective responsibilities, who shall not place on the market, put into service, or use AI systems that make risk assessments to assess or predict the risk of a natural person committing a criminal offence based solely on profiling of a natural person or on assessing their personality traits and characteristics.
obligations.scope: Providers and deployers of AI systems used for individual criminal risk assessment or crime prediction

obligations.source_paragraph: Article 5(1)(e) AI Act (as interpreted in section 6.2, paragraph 225)
obligations.requirement_text: Providers and deployers of AI systems shall not place on the market, put into service, or use AI systems that create or expand facial recognition databases through the untargeted scraping of facial images from the internet or CCTV footage.
obligations.scope: Providers and deployers of AI systems used to create or expand facial recognition databases through untargeted scraping of facial images

obligations.requirement_text: Providers and deployers of AI systems shall not place on the market, put into service, or use AI systems to infer emotions of a natural person in the areas of workplace and education institutions, except where the use of the AI system is intended for medical or safety reasons.
obligations.scope: Providers and deployers of AI systems used to infer emotions in the workplace or education institutions

obligations.requirement_text: Providers and deployers of AI systems shall not place on the market, put into service, or use biometric categorisation systems that categorise individually natural persons based on their biometric data to deduce or infer their race, political opinions, trade union membership, religious or philosophical beliefs, sex-life, or sexual orientation.
obligations.scope: Providers and deployers of biometric categorisation AI systems

obligations.requirement_text: Providers and deployers shall not use real-time remote biometric identification systems in publicly accessible spaces for law enforcement purposes, except where necessary for the targeted search of specific victims, the prevention of specific threats including terrorist attacks, or the search of suspects of specific offences, subject to further procedural requirements including for authorisation outlined in Article 5(2)-(7) AI Act.
obligations.scope: Providers and deployers of real-time remote biometric identification systems used in publicly accessible spaces for law enforcement purposes in the Union

obligations.requirement_text: Providers must ensure their AI systems meet all relevant requirements before placing them on the market or putting them into service.
obligations.scope: Providers of AI systems placed on the market or put into service in the Union, including providers established outside the Union
obligations.deadline: before placing on the market or putting into service

obligations.requirement_text: Providers and deployers of AI systems shall continuously comply with the AI Act during all phases of the AI lifecycle, including ongoing monitoring of and updates to AI systems placed on the market or put into service in the Union, to ensure that an AI system remains compliant with the AI Act throughout its lifecycle and does not result in a practice prohibited under Article 5 AI Act.
obligations.scope: Providers and deployers of AI systems placed on the market or put into service in the Union
obligations.evidence_required: Records of ongoing monitoring and updates to AI systems demonstrating continued compliance throughout the AI system lifecycle

obligations.requirement_text: Where an AI system placed on the market, put into service, or used for military, defence, or national security purposes is also used (temporarily or permanently) for other purposes such as civilian, humanitarian, law enforcement, or public security purposes, the entity using the AI system for those other purposes shall ensure compliance of the AI system with the AI Act before such use, unless the system already complies.
obligations.scope: Entities (providers or deployers) using AI systems for non-excluded purposes in the Union, where those systems are also used for military, defence, or national security purposes
obligations.evidence_required: Verification that the AI system complies with the AI Act prior to use for non-excluded purposes

obligations.requirement_text: Providers of 'dual use' AI systems placed on the market or put into service for both an excluded purpose (military, defence, or national security) and one or more non-excluded purposes (such as civilian or law enforcement purposes) shall ensure that those systems comply with the requirements in the AI Act.
obligations.scope: Providers of dual-use AI systems placed on the market or put into service in the Union
obligations.evidence_required: Documentation demonstrating compliance of the dual-use AI system with AI Act requirements

obligations.requirement_text: A company that offers a dual-use AI system (including for law enforcement and national security purposes) as the provider of that system must ensure its compliance with the requirements in the AI Act.
obligations.scope: Providers of AI systems with dual uses, including law enforcement and national security

obligations.requirement_text: Where an AI system is placed on the market or put into service as a result of research and development activity, operators must comply with the AI Act.
obligations.scope: Providers placing AI systems on the market or putting them into service following R&D activity
obligations.deadline: before placing on the market or putting into service

obligations.requirement_text: Real-world testing of AI systems must be fully compliant with the requirements for the relevant AI systems in the AI Act, unless the system is tested in an AI regulatory sandbox or in accordance with the special regime for testing in real-world conditions outside the sandbox as provided for in Articles 60 and 61 AI Act.
obligations.scope: Any operator conducting real-world testing of AI systems

obligations.requirement_text: Natural persons using an AI system for professional activities (e.g. freelancers, journalists, doctors) must comply with the obligations for deployers under the AI Act.
obligations.scope: Deployers who are natural persons using AI systems for professional activities

obligations.requirement_text: Providers of AI systems released under free and open-source licences cannot benefit from the exclusion in Article 2(12) AI Act if the AI system they place on the market or put into service constitutes a prohibited practice under Article 5 AI Act.
obligations.scope: Providers of AI systems released under free and open-source licences that constitute prohibited practices under Article 5 AI Act

obligations.requirement_text: Each operator shall take measures for which they are best placed based on their role and control over the system in the value chain to ensure a responsible and safe provision and use of AI systems, balancing their risks and benefits.
obligations.scope: All operators (providers, deployers, importers, distributors) across the AI value chain

obligations.requirement_text: Deployers must not use any AI system in a manner prohibited under Article 5 AI Act, including not bypassing any safety guardrails implemented by the providers of the system.
obligations.scope: Deployers of any AI system

obligations.requirement_text: Providers must not place on the market or put into service AI systems, including general-purpose AI systems, that are reasonably likely to behave or be directly used in a manner prohibited by Article 5 AI Act.
obligations.scope: Providers of any AI system, including general-purpose AI systems
obligations.deadline: before placing on the market or putting into service

obligations.requirement_text: Providers are expected to take effective and verifiable measures to build in safeguards and prevent and mitigate harmful behaviour and misuse to the extent they are reasonably foreseeable and measures are feasible and proportionate.
obligations.scope: Providers of any AI system, including general-purpose AI systems
obligations.deadline: before placing on the market or putting into service
obligations.evidence_required: Documentation of effective and verifiable safeguards and measures to prevent and mitigate harmful behaviour and misuse

obligations.requirement_text: In their contractual relationships with deployers (i.e. in the terms of use of the AI system), providers are expected to exclude use of their AI system for prohibited practices and provide appropriate information in the instructions of use for deployers and regarding the necessary human oversight.
obligations.scope: Providers of any AI system in their contractual relationships with deployers
obligations.evidence_required: Terms of use explicitly excluding prohibited practices; instructions of use providing appropriate information and human oversight guidance

obligations.requirement_text: Where providers have limited possibilities to integrate preventive and mitigating measures, they must primarily provide appropriate instructions and information to deployers and the required human oversight and restrict prohibited use of the system.
obligations.scope: Providers of AI systems where prohibitions are linked to a very specific purpose of the system
obligations.evidence_required: Instructions of use; information provided to deployers; documentation of restrictions on prohibited use

obligations.requirement_text: Providers are expected to take appropriate measures if they become aware that the system is misused for a specific prohibited purpose by specific deployers.
obligations.scope: Providers of AI systems upon becoming aware of misuse by deployers

obligations.requirement_text: Member States must designate their competent market surveillance authorities by 2 August 2025.
obligations.scope: Member States

obligations.requirement_text: Where there are cross-border implications beyond the territory of the market surveillance authority, the authority of the Member State concerned must inform the Commission and the market surveillance authorities of other Member States.
obligations.scope: Market surveillance authorities of Member States where AI systems present cross-border risks

obligations.requirement_text: All market surveillance authorities shall follow a Union safeguard procedure with a decision taken by the Commission determining whether the AI system constitutes a prohibited practice, to ensure prohibitions are applied uniformly across all Member States.
obligations.scope: Market surveillance authorities in all Member States

obligations.requirement_text: Deployers shall label 'deep fakes' and certain AI-generated text publications on matters of public interest.
obligations.scope: Deployers of AI systems generating deep fakes or AI-generated text on matters of public interest
obligations.evidence_required: Visible labelling applied to deep fakes and AI-generated content publications on matters of public interest

obligations.requirement_text: Providers shall ensure AI systems interacting with people are designed in a way to inform people that they are interacting with AI and not a human.
obligations.scope: Providers of AI systems that interact with natural persons
obligations.evidence_required: Design documentation demonstrating disclosure features informing users they are interacting with AI

obligations.requirement_text: Providers shall enable visible disclosure through design features embedded in the AI system, including technical measures enabling the detection of AI-generated and manipulated content.
obligations.scope: Providers of AI systems capable of generating or manipulating content
obligations.evidence_required: Technical documentation showing embedded design features and technical measures enabling detection of AI-generated and manipulated content

obligations.requirement_text: Providers and deployers of AI systems that deploy manipulative or deceptive techniques shall implement appropriate preventive and mitigating measures to avoid or mitigate the risk of significant harms that are reasonably likely to result from those techniques.
obligations.scope: Providers and deployers of AI systems deploying subliminal, purposefully manipulative, or deceptive techniques
obligations.evidence_required: Records of preventive and mitigating measures implemented to address reasonably foreseeable significant harms

obligations.requirement_text: Providers and deployers of AI systems that deploy manipulative or deceptive techniques are encouraged to provide transparency in how the AI system operates, clear disclosures about its capabilities and limitations, and relevant information to ensure informed decisions; respect individual autonomy; and integrate appropriate user control and safeguard measures to ensure the system is not deceptive and operates within the boundaries of lawful persuasion.
obligations.scope: Providers and deployers of AI systems deploying subliminal, purposefully manipulative, or deceptive techniques
obligations.evidence_required: Transparency disclosures, user-facing information about capabilities and limitations, and documentation of user control and safeguard measures

obligations.requirement_text: Market surveillance authorities must investigate each case's specific facts and circumstances, assessing whether the subliminal, purposefully manipulative or deceptive technique deployed by the AI system is likely to appreciably impair the decision-making, individual autonomy and free choice of an 'average' individual within a targeted group when the system affects a group of persons in a manner that is reasonably likely to cause significant harm.
obligations.scope: Market surveillance authorities
obligations.evidence_required: Case investigation records and assessment documentation covering specific facts, circumstances, and impact on average individual's decision-making autonomy

obligations.requirement_text: The placing on the market, the putting into service or the use of an AI system that exploits any of the vulnerabilities of a natural person or a specific group of persons due to their age, disability or a specific social or economic situation, with the objective, or the effect, of materially distorting the behaviour of that person or a person belonging to that group in a manner that causes or is reasonably likely to cause that person or another person significant harm shall be prohibited.
obligations.scope: Providers and deployers of AI systems — applies to any AI system placed on the market, put into service, or used in a manner that exploits vulnerabilities related to age, disability, or specific social or economic situation

obligations.requirement_text: Providers or deployers of AI systems that are aware that their systems unlawfully discriminate against persons or groups of persons in specific social or economic situations and have not taken appropriate corrective measures shall be considered to exploit those persons' vulnerabilities where they are aware of the reasonably likely significant harm that those persons are likely to suffer.
obligations.scope: Providers and deployers of AI systems whose systems unlawfully discriminate against persons in specific social or economic situations
obligations.evidence_required: Documentation of corrective measures taken upon becoming aware of unlawful discriminatory impact on socially or economically disadvantaged persons

obligations.source_paragraph: Article 5(1)(c) AI Act (as interpreted in paragraph 171)
obligations.requirement_text: In the case of checks by competent market surveillance authorities, the provider and the deployer of the AI system, each within their responsibilities, shall demonstrate that the AI practice is legitimate and justified, including by providing transparency of the functioning of the AI system, and information about the types of data and data sources, ensuring that only data related to the social context in which the score is used are processed for the purpose of the evaluation or classification, the system is performing as intended, and any resulting detrimental or unfavourable treatment is justified and proportionate to the social behaviour.
obligations.scope: Providers and deployers of AI systems used for evaluation or classification of persons (social scoring context) under Article 5(1)(c) AI Act
obligations.evidence_required: Documentation of AI system functioning, types of data and data sources used, evidence that only contextually related data is processed, evidence that detrimental/unfavourable treatment is justified and proportionate

obligations.source_paragraph: Article 5(1)(c) AI Act (as interpreted in paragraph 178)
obligations.requirement_text: Providers and deployers shall carefully assess whether other applicable Union and national legislation applies to any particular AI scoring system used in their activities, in particular if there is more specific legislation that strictly regulates the types of data that can be used as relevant and necessary for specific evaluation purposes and if there are more specific rules and procedures to ensure justified and fair treatment.
obligations.scope: Providers and deployers of AI scoring systems
obligations.evidence_required: Records of legal assessment identifying applicable Union and national legislation relevant to the AI scoring system

obligations.source_paragraph: Article 5(1)(d) AI Act (as interpreted in section 5.2.3, paragraph 204)
obligations.requirement_text: Where an AI system falls within the exclusion from the prohibition in Article 5(1)(d) and is classified as a high-risk AI system, it shall be subject to the requirements and safeguards including human oversight under Article 14 and Article 26 AI Act; the human oversight must be assigned to persons with the necessary competence, training and authority who should be able to properly understand the capabilities and limitations of the AI system, correctly interpret its output and address the risk of automation bias.
obligations.scope: Deployers (primarily law enforcement authorities) of high-risk AI systems used to support human assessment of individual crime risk, as referred to in Annex III, point 6(d), AI Act
obligations.evidence_required: Documentation of human oversight procedures, training records, and designation of persons with necessary competence and authority to assess AI outputs and intervene when necessary

obligations.source_paragraph: Article 5(1)(d) AI Act (as interpreted in section 5.2.3, paragraph 204)
obligations.requirement_text: Persons assigned to human oversight of high-risk AI systems used for crime risk assessment shall have clear procedures, training and the necessary competence and authority to meaningfully assess the outputs of the AI system, and their human assessment must ensure that any AI prediction or assessment of the risk of a person committing a crime is based on objective and verifiable facts linked to a criminal activity; those persons shall also intervene in order to avoid negative consequences or risks, or stop the use of the AI system if it does not perform as intended.
obligations.scope: Deployers (primarily law enforcement authorities) of high-risk AI systems used for individual crime risk assessment falling under Annex III, point 6(d), AI Act
obligations.evidence_required: Documented procedures for human oversight, training records, evidence of interventions where AI system did not perform as intended

obligations.source_paragraph: Article 5(1)(e) AI Act (as interpreted in section 6.2.2, paragraph 231)
obligations.requirement_text: Where systems combine targeted searches for images or videos with untargeted searches, the untargeted scraping is prohibited; providers and deployers shall not place on the market, put into service, or use such combined systems insofar as they include untargeted scraping components.
obligations.scope: Providers and deployers of AI systems combining targeted and untargeted scraping of facial images for facial recognition databases

obligations.source_paragraph: Article 5(1)(e) AI Act (as interpreted in section 6.3, paragraph 236)
obligations.requirement_text: Existing facial databases built up prior to the entry into application of the prohibition, which are not further expanded through AI-enabled untargeted scraping, must comply with the applicable Union data protection rules.
obligations.scope: Any party operating existing facial recognition databases not further expanded by AI-enabled untargeted scraping
obligations.evidence_required: Documentation of compliance with applicable Union data protection rules for pre-existing facial recognition databases

obligations.requirement_text: Any use of emotion recognition systems for medical and safety reasons shall always remain limited to what is strictly necessary and proportionate, including limits in time, personal application and scale, and shall be accompanied by sufficient safeguards, such as prior written and motivated expert opinion relating to the specific use case.
obligations.scope: Deployers (employers and educational institutions) using emotion recognition AI systems for medical or safety reasons
obligations.evidence_required: Prior written and motivated expert opinion relating to the specific use case; documentation of necessity assessment and proportionality limits

obligations.requirement_text: Employers and educators shall only deploy emotion recognition systems for medical and safety reasons in case of an explicit need, and data collected and processed in this context may not be used for any other purpose.
obligations.scope: Deployers (employers and educational institutions) using emotion recognition AI systems under the medical/safety exception
obligations.evidence_required: Documentation of explicit need and records demonstrating data is not used for purposes other than the declared medical or safety reason

obligations.source_paragraph: Article 5(1)(f) and Article 6(2), Annex III(1)(c), Article 50(3), paragraph (263)
obligations.requirement_text: Emotion recognition systems that classify as high-risk systems pursuant to Article 6(2) and Annex III(1)(c) AI Act shall comply with the high-risk requirements in Chapter III Section 2 AI Act and the transparency obligation of Article 50(3) AI Act.
obligations.scope: Providers and deployers of emotion recognition AI systems classified as high-risk under Article 6(2) and Annex III(1)(c)
obligations.evidence_required: Compliance documentation for Chapter III Section 2 high-risk requirements; transparency disclosures as required by Article 50(3)

obligations.requirement_text: Deployers of crowd-control or analogous systems operating in contexts where employees may incidentally be screened must employ safeguards to avoid the screening of employees.
obligations.scope: Deployers of AI systems used for crowd control or security screening in contexts where employees may also be present
obligations.evidence_required: Documentation of safeguards implemented to prevent employee emotion screening

obligations.requirement_text: The use of 'real-time' remote biometric identification (RBI) systems in publicly accessible spaces for the purpose of law enforcement shall be prohibited, unless and in so far as such use is strictly necessary for one of the three exhaustively listed objectives (targeted search for victims of abduction, trafficking or sexual exploitation or missing persons; prevention of a specific, substantial and imminent threat to life or physical safety or a terrorist attack; localisation or identification of a suspect of a serious criminal offence listed in Annex II).
obligations.scope: Deployers of real-time RBI systems used in publicly accessible spaces for law enforcement purposes, including law enforcement authorities and entities acting on their behalf.

obligations.source_paragraph: Article 5(2)–(7) AI Act (as referenced in Article 5(1)(h) and paragraphs 289, 294, 326)
obligations.requirement_text: Where the use of real-time RBI systems in publicly accessible spaces for law enforcement purposes is permitted under one of the three listed objectives, such use must comply with the conditions and safeguards laid down in Article 5(2) to (7) AI Act, and must be authorised by national legislation.
obligations.scope: Deployers of real-time RBI systems used in publicly accessible spaces for law enforcement purposes (law enforcement authorities and entities acting on their behalf).
obligations.evidence_required: National legislative authorisation; documentation demonstrating compliance with the safeguards and conditions of Article 5(2)–(7) AI Act.

obligations.source_paragraph: Article 5(5) AI Act (as referenced in paragraph 290)
obligations.requirement_text: In the absence of national legislation allowing and regulating the use of real-time RBI systems in publicly accessible spaces for law enforcement purposes, law enforcement authorities and entities acting on their behalf may not deploy such systems for law enforcement purposes.
obligations.scope: Law enforcement authorities and entities acting on their behalf seeking to deploy real-time RBI systems in publicly accessible spaces.
obligations.evidence_required: Existence of applicable national legislation authorising such use.

obligations.source_paragraph: Article 6(2) and Annex III, point (a) AI Act (as referenced in paragraph 291)
obligations.requirement_text: The placing on the market and the putting into service of real-time RBI systems, as well as the use of other (non-real-time) RBI systems, is not prohibited but must comply with the rules for high-risk AI systems.
obligations.scope: Providers and deployers of RBI systems (other than the prohibited real-time use in publicly accessible spaces for law enforcement).
obligations.evidence_required: Documentation demonstrating compliance with high-risk AI system requirements under Chapter III AI Act.

obligations.source_paragraph: Article 26(10) AI Act (as referenced in paragraphs 292 and 284 footnote)
obligations.requirement_text: The retrospective (non-real-time) use of RBI systems for law enforcement purposes is not prohibited, but is subject to additional safeguards for the deployment of high-risk AI systems as laid down in Article 26(10) AI Act.
obligations.scope: Deployers using retrospective RBI systems for law enforcement purposes.
obligations.evidence_required: Documentation demonstrating compliance with the additional safeguards required by Article 26(10) AI Act.

obligations.source_paragraph: Article 5(1)(h)(i) AI Act (as referenced in paragraphs 329, 331–334)
obligations.requirement_text: Where real-time RBI is used in publicly accessible spaces for law enforcement purposes under the first exception (targeted search for victims of abduction, trafficking, sexual exploitation, or missing persons), the deployer must define a perimeter of deployment and duration of use and must ensure the use is strictly necessary.
obligations.scope: Law enforcement authorities and entities acting on their behalf deploying real-time RBI systems under the first exception of Article 5(1)(h)(i) AI Act.
obligations.evidence_required: Documentation defining the perimeter and duration of deployment; evidence of strict necessity.

obligations.requirement_text: The use of 'real-time' remote biometric identification systems in publicly accessible spaces for the purposes of law enforcement shall be deployed only to confirm the identity of the specifically targeted individual, and shall take into account: (a) the nature of the situation giving rise to the possible use, in particular the seriousness, probability and scale of the harm that would be caused if the system were not used; and (b) the consequences of the use of the system for the rights and freedoms of all persons concerned, in particular the seriousness, probability and scale of those consequences.
obligations.scope: Law enforcement authorities deploying real-time remote biometric identification (RBI) systems in publicly accessible spaces under Article 5(1)(h) AI Act
obligations.evidence_required: Documentation demonstrating deployment was limited to confirming identity of specifically targeted individual; records of assessment of seriousness, probability and scale of harm and consequences

obligations.requirement_text: The use of 'real-time' remote biometric identification systems in publicly accessible spaces for law enforcement purposes shall comply with necessary and proportionate safeguards and conditions in relation to the use in accordance with the national law authorising the use thereof, in particular as regards the temporal, geographic and personal limitations.
obligations.scope: Law enforcement authorities deploying real-time RBI systems in publicly accessible spaces under Article 5(1)(h) AI Act
obligations.evidence_required: Records demonstrating compliance with temporal, geographic, and personal limitations as set out in authorising national law

obligations.requirement_text: The use of the 'real-time' remote biometric identification system in publicly accessible spaces shall be authorised only if the law enforcement authority has completed a fundamental rights impact assessment as provided for in Article 27 and has registered the system in the EU database according to Article 49. In duly justified cases of urgency, the use of such systems may be commenced without registration in the EU database, provided that such registration is completed without undue delay.
obligations.scope: Law enforcement authorities deploying real-time RBI systems in publicly accessible spaces under Article 5(1)(h) AI Act
obligations.deadline: Before deployment (registration without undue delay in urgent cases)
obligations.evidence_required: Completed Fundamental Rights Impact Assessment (FRIA) pursuant to Article 27; registration record in the EU database pursuant to Article 49

obligations.requirement_text: In the period after Article 5 AI Act becomes applicable (after 2 February 2025) but before provisions on high-risk AI systems are applicable (before 2 August 2026), deployers of real-time RBI systems meeting the conditions to benefit from exceptions in Article 5(1)(h) AI Act shall implement the requirements for FRIA set out in Article 27 AI Act.
obligations.scope: Deployers of real-time RBI systems in publicly accessible spaces for law enforcement purposes during the interim period (2 February 2025 – 2 August 2026)
obligations.evidence_required: Completed FRIA meeting the requirements of Article 27 AI Act

obligations.requirement_text: The obligation to carry out the FRIA under Article 5(2) AI Act is imposed on the deployers of the RBI system. If other actors are acting on behalf of the deployer/the law enforcement authority, they shall contribute to the preparation of the FRIA with all relevant information to ensure it is properly carried out.
obligations.scope: Deployers of real-time RBI systems (and entities acting on their behalf) in publicly accessible spaces for law enforcement purposes
obligations.deadline: Before deployment of the authorised real-time RBI system
obligations.evidence_required: Completed FRIA; records of contributions from entities acting on behalf of the deployer

obligations.requirement_text: A FRIA must be carried out before the deployment of the authorised real-time RBI system.
obligations.scope: Deployers of real-time RBI systems in publicly accessible spaces for law enforcement purposes
obligations.evidence_required: Completed and dated FRIA predating the deployment

obligations.requirement_text: A FRIA shall include: a description of the RBI use and the deployer's processes for the use together with the intended purpose; the period of use and frequency of use; the categories of persons and groups affected by the system; and the specific risks of harm to the affected persons.
obligations.scope: Deployers of real-time RBI systems in publicly accessible spaces for law enforcement purposes
obligations.evidence_required: FRIA document containing all required elements: description of use and processes, period and frequency of use, categories of affected persons, and specific risks of harm

obligations.source_paragraph: Article 5(3) AI Act; section 10.1.1, paragraph 376 (human oversight measures)
obligations.requirement_text: The FRIA shall describe the procedures that will be followed during the operation of the system and how the output will be interpreted in the context of the decision-making process, including the role of a human agent in verifying and interpreting the output, and training to operate the system. No decision that would adversely affect an individual may be taken solely on the basis of the output of the real-time RBI system.
obligations.scope: Deployers of real-time RBI systems in publicly accessible spaces for law enforcement purposes
obligations.evidence_required: FRIA section describing human oversight procedures; training records for operators; documentation that no adverse decision is based solely on system output

obligations.requirement_text: Beyond implementing human oversight measures, the deployer shall explain redress measures in case a risk materialises, including the governance procedures and the complaint mechanisms (such as in the case of a misidentification).
obligations.scope: Deployers of real-time RBI systems in publicly accessible spaces for law enforcement purposes
obligations.evidence_required: FRIA section or separate documentation describing redress measures, governance procedures, and complaint mechanisms

obligations.requirement_text: The deployer of a real-time RBI system used in publicly accessible spaces for law enforcement purposes shall register the system in the EU database provided for in Article 49 AI Act before deployment. In cases of a duly justified emergency, deployment may start prior to registration, provided the law enforcement authority registers the system without undue delay (as soon as possible given the circumstances).
obligations.scope: Deployers of real-time RBI systems in publicly accessible spaces for law enforcement purposes
obligations.deadline: Before deployment (or without undue delay after deployment in emergency cases)
obligations.evidence_required: Registration record in the EU database pursuant to Article 49 AI Act; in emergency cases, documentation of the emergency justification and timestamp of registration

obligations.requirement_text: Each individual use of a real-time RBI system in publicly accessible spaces for law enforcement purposes shall be subject to a prior authorisation granted by a judicial authority or an independent administrative authority whose decision is binding of the Member State in which the use is to take place, issued upon a reasoned request and in accordance with the detailed rules of national law. In a duly justified situation of urgency, use may commence without authorisation provided that such authorisation is requested without undue delay, at the latest within 24 hours.
obligations.scope: Law enforcement authorities deploying real-time RBI systems in publicly accessible spaces
obligations.deadline: Prior to each individual deployment (or within 24 hours of deployment in urgent cases)
obligations.evidence_required: Prior authorisation decision from a judicial or independent administrative authority; in urgent cases, record of authorisation request submitted within 24 hours

obligations.requirement_text: If the prior authorisation is rejected, the use of the real-time RBI system shall be stopped with immediate effect and all data, as well as the results and outputs of that use, shall be immediately discarded and deleted.
obligations.scope: Law enforcement authorities deploying real-time RBI systems in publicly accessible spaces
obligations.evidence_required: Records confirming immediate cessation of use and deletion of all data, results, and outputs upon rejection of authorisation

obligations.requirement_text: The competent judicial authority or independent administrative authority shall grant the authorisation only where it is satisfied, on the basis of objective evidence or clear indications presented to it, that the use of the real-time RBI system is necessary for, and proportionate to, achieving one of the objectives specified in Article 5(1)(h), and in particular remains limited to what is strictly necessary concerning the period of time as well as the geographic and personal scope.
obligations.scope: Judicial authorities or independent administrative authorities authorising real-time RBI systems for law enforcement purposes
obligations.evidence_required: Reasoned authorisation decision referencing objective evidence or clear indications of necessity and proportionality; specification of temporal, geographic, and personal scope limitations

obligations.requirement_text: No decision that produces an adverse legal effect on a person may be taken based solely on the output of the 'real-time' remote biometric identification system.
obligations.scope: Deployers of real-time remote biometric identification systems in publicly accessible spaces for law enforcement purposes
obligations.evidence_required: Documentation of decision-making processes demonstrating that adverse legal decisions are not based solely on RBI system output

obligations.requirement_text: Each use of a 'real-time' remote biometric identification system in publicly accessible spaces for law enforcement purposes shall be notified to the relevant market surveillance authority and the national data protection authority in accordance with the national rules referred to in paragraph 5. The notification shall, as a minimum, contain the information specified under paragraph 6 and shall not include sensitive operational data.
obligations.scope: Deployers (law enforcement authorities) using real-time remote biometric identification systems in publicly accessible spaces for law enforcement purposes
obligations.evidence_required: Notification records submitted to the relevant market surveillance authority and national data protection authority, containing the minimum required information and excluding sensitive operational data

obligations.requirement_text: Member States that adopt national laws authorising the use of real-time remote biometric identification systems in publicly accessible spaces for law enforcement purposes shall lay down in their national law the necessary detailed rules for the request, issuance and exercise of, as well as supervision and reporting relating to, the authorisations referred to in paragraph 3, including specification of which objectives and criminal offences the competent authorities may be authorised to use those systems for.
obligations.scope: Member States adopting national laws on real-time RBI for law enforcement
obligations.evidence_required: National law text containing the required detailed rules

obligations.requirement_text: Member States shall notify the national rules on the use of real-time remote biometric identification systems to the Commission at the latest 30 days following the adoption thereof.
obligations.scope: Member States that adopt national laws authorising real-time RBI for law enforcement
obligations.deadline: at the latest 30 days following the adoption of the national law
obligations.evidence_required: Notification submitted to the Commission within the 30-day deadline

obligations.requirement_text: National market surveillance authorities and national data protection authorities of Member States that have been notified of the use of 'real-time' remote biometric identification systems in publicly accessible spaces for law enforcement purposes shall submit to the Commission annual reports on such use, based on a template provided by the Commission. Where the deployer is an EU institution, body, or agency, the EDPS is obliged to inform the Commission accordingly on an annual basis.
obligations.scope: National market surveillance authorities and national data protection authorities of Member States; European Data Protection Supervisor (for EU institutions, bodies, or agencies)
obligations.evidence_required: Annual reports submitted to the Commission based on the Commission-provided template, covering the required information on authorisations and their results

obligations.requirement_text: The Commission shall publish annual reports on the use of real-time remote biometric identification systems in publicly accessible spaces for law enforcement purposes, based on aggregated data in Member States on the basis of the annual reports referred to in paragraph 6. Those annual reports shall not include sensitive operational data of the related law enforcement activities.
obligations.scope: The European Commission
obligations.evidence_required: Published annual reports based on aggregated data from Member States, excluding sensitive operational data

obligations.requirement_text: Pursuant to Article 14(5) AI Act, no action or decision may be taken by the deployer on the basis of the identification resulting from a real-time RBI system, unless that identification has been separately verified and confirmed by at least two natural persons with the necessary competence, training and authority, or unless Union or national law considers the application of this requirement to be disproportionate.
obligations.scope: Deployers of real-time remote biometric identification systems (high-risk AI systems under Annex III, point 1(a))
obligations.evidence_required: Documentation showing that identification results were verified and confirmed by at least two qualified natural persons before any action or decision was taken

obligations.requirement_text: Article 4 AI Act prescribes AI literacy measures for providers and users of AI systems to ensure a sufficient level of AI literacy of their staff and other persons dealing with the operation and use of AI systems, considering the persons on whom the systems are to be used.
obligations.scope: Providers and deployers (users) of AI systems, including real-time RBI systems
obligations.evidence_required: Records of AI literacy training and measures implemented for staff and persons dealing with the operation and use of AI systems

obligations.requirement_text: Providers and deployers of AI systems shall take necessary measures to ensure that they do not place on the market, put into service, or use AI systems that could constitute prohibited practices under Article 5 AI Act, which applies as from 2 February 2025 regardless of whether systems were placed on the market or put into service before or after that date.
obligations.scope: Providers and deployers of AI systems subject to Article 5 AI Act prohibitions
obligations.evidence_required: Documentation of measures taken to assess and ensure AI systems in the portfolio do not constitute prohibited practices under Article 5 AI Act
